title: feat(fireworks,groq,openrouter): add standard model property
body_text: Add a `model` property to `ChatFireworks`, `ChatGroq`, and `ChatOpenRouter` that returns `model_name`. These partners use Pydantic's `Field(alias="model")` on `model_name`, which means `instance.model` doesn't work as a read accessor after construction — it raises an `AttributeError` or returns the field descriptor. `ChatOpenAI` already has this property; this brings the remaining in-repo partners to parity.

entities.name: Model Property
entities.description: A property named 'model' that returns the 'model_name'.

entities.description: The underlying field storing the model's name.

entities.name: Pydantic
entities.description: A data validation library used by the chat model classes.

entities.name: Field Alias
entities.description: Pydantic's `Field(alias="model")` mechanism used for aliasing `model_name` to `model`.

entities.name: AttributeError
entities.description: An error raised when attempting to access `instance.model` as a read accessor.

entities.name: ChatOpenAI
entities.description: Another chat model class that already has the 'model' property.

relations.label: CAUSES
relations.evidence: means `instance.model` doesn't work as a read accessor after construction — it raises an `AttributeError`

extracted_data.summary: The issue proposes adding a `model` property to `ChatFireworks`, `ChatGroq`, and `ChatOpenRouter` to return `model_name`, addressing an `AttributeError` caused by Pydantic's `Field(alias="model")` and bringing them to parity with `ChatOpenAI`.
